title: The Five Koshas: A Practical Framework for Wholeness
title_es: Los Cinco Koshas: Un Marco Práctico para la Plenitud
subtitle_en: Satchidananda — Escuela de Yoga Satyam, Uruguay — February 9, 2026
subtitle_es: Satchidananda — Escuela de Yoga Satyam, Uruguay — 9 de febrero de 2026
subtitle_ne: सत्चिदानन्द — Escuela de Yoga Satyam, Uruguay — फेब्रुअरी ९, २०२६
description_en: If you don't have that map or key for yourself, then you have to question: for how long are you just going to follow someone else's instruction?
abstract_en: Imagine a four-legged table — each leg a kosha. Miss one and the table still stands, until life places weight on that corner. Satchidananda explains three ways the table of our life can fail — missing leg, uneven legs, a leg that quietly reaches its limit — each with a real-life parallel. "If that has happened to you, please measure the legs of your tables."

title: Living Fully In Yourself
subtitle_en: Satchidananda — Clínica Vitola, Yoga Carrasco, Uruguay — February 10, 2026
subtitle_es: Satchidananda — Clínica Vitola, Yoga Carrasco, Uruguay — 10 de febrero de 2026
subtitle_ne: सत्चिदानन्द — Clínica Vitola, Yoga Carrasco, Uruguay — फेब्रुअरी १०, २०२६
description_en: Twenty years of studying the koshas, but life changes have only happened in the past five — because that's when I really had to apply the theory.
abstract_en: "What if our boundaries aren't limited to matter or our physical body? Then what does it mean to be full in the next dimension of our being?" To inhabit yourself fully means developing conscious contact with all five layers — not just the physical. When you can sense imbalance across energetic, mental, and intuitive dimensions before crisis hits, you gain the ability to respond rather than react.

title: The Levels of the Human Being According to Yoga and Their Dialogue with AI
title_es: Los Niveles del Ser Humano según el Yoga y su Diálogo con la IA
title_ne: योग अनुसार मानव अस्तित्वका स्तरहरू र एआईसँग तिनीहरूको संवाद
subtitle_en: Satchidananda — MACA Museum, Organized by Yoga 104, Uruguay — February 11, 2026
subtitle_es: Satchidananda — Museo MACA, Organizado por Yoga 104, Uruguay — 11 de febrero de 2026
subtitle_ne: सत्चिदानन्द — MACA Museum, Yoga 104 द्वारा आयोजित, Uruguay — फेब्रुअरी ११, २०२६
description_en: At Montevideo's MACA Museum, a guided journey through the five koshas exploring where human consciousness and artificial intelligence meet.
abstract_en: "To me it's less important whether AI is conscious or not — and more significant: if I am a medium of consciousness, how do I become the clearest medium possible?" As AI replicates what we assumed was uniquely human, the koshas reveal where our true nature actually lives. Rather than fear obsolescence, yoga invites us to stop over-identifying with any single layer and step into the power of the whole self — all five koshas.

title: Tantric Tools: Tantroktam Devi Suktam for Self-Reflection
title_es: Herramientas Tántricas: Tantroktam Devi Suktam para la Autorreflexión
title_ne: तान्त्रिक उपकरणहरू: आत्म-चिन्तनका लागि तन्त्रोक्तम् देवी सूक्तम्
title_short_es: Tantroktam Devi Suktam para la Autorreflexión
subtitle_en: Satchidananda — SitaRam Darshan, La Paloma — February 18, 2026
subtitle_es: Satchidananda — SitaRam Darshan, La Paloma — 18 de febrero de 2026
subtitle_ne: सत्चिदानन्द — SitaRam Darshan, La Paloma — फेब्रुअरी १८, २०२६
description_en: Tantroktam Devi Suktam: a tantric tool for self-reflection, mapping Shakti's faces in our own consciousness — the faces we rotate through and feel deeply.
abstract_en: The Tantroktam Devi Suktam is not poetry — it's a practical technology. Each verse invokes a specific energy already latent within us. From a grandmother's nighttime mantra to warrior goddess imagery, Shakti's forms — creative, destructive, transformative — are not external divinity but our own faces. This is the practice of learning to rotate through them, feel them fully, and move consciously between states.

title: The Koshas: A Map of the Inner World
title_es: Los Koshas: Un Mapa del Mundo Interior
title_short: The Koshas: A Map of the Inner World
title_short_es: Los Koshas: Un Mapa del Mundo Interior
subtitle_es: Satchidananda — Piriápolis, Uruguay — 23 de febrero de 2026
description_en: The five koshas as a living, overlapping map of inner reality — traversed first through guided practice, then through our own familiarity.
abstract_en: "At what point do we stop being us?" By systematically removing layers — sensation, energy, thought, emotion — yogic inquiry reveals the witness that remains. "Like any treasure hunter, it's good to have a map — and a precise map is much better than an imprecise one." When you learn to navigate your inner dimensions with that precision, no external storm can fully take you down.
